key_facts: Dashboard tracks Taostats subnet 110 using plain JavaScript Node.js and local SQLite.
Live poll intervals are limited to 60/120/240 minutes, persisted in SQLite app_settings, and can be changed from the admin drawer when TAOSTATS_ADMIN_API_KEY is configured.
The top bar shows the current TAO price used for USD conversion and the next scheduled poll time as a local timestamp.
The dashboard now opens with a wallet section first, followed by a collapsible financial perspective panel that explains price, flow, sentiment, and supply pressure in plain language.
The dashboard also includes a 'What matters most today' panel that highlights the main correlation points for beginners.
The financial perspective area now also includes a watchlist panel that keeps the core alerts (price vs flow, sentiment watch, supply watch) visible in a stable beginner-friendly format.

The dashboard can now track multiple configured wallet balances from Taostats account latest/history endpoints using coldkeys, optional hotkeys, optional hotkey ROLE tags for validator/owner attribution, and wallet names from .env, and the wallet modal now also shows a compact wallet profile with rank, created-on date, hotkey summary, estimated income sources, current subnet stake positions, and a collapsible hotkey history section backed by historical stake snapshots with delta color-coding.

The wallet modal previously regressed because renderWalletDetails referenced a nonexistent walletStakeHistory variable; keep the browser modal using state.modalStakeHistory and guard wallet-specific rendering so a hotkey-history issue cannot blank the main wallet chart.

The dashboard now includes a standalone Pool growth estimator section directly underneath the wallet section; it uses the latest local subnet pool snapshot, simulates TAO injection locally, and must fail closed if the pool reserves or price are missing.

The pool growth estimator now also shows implied projected subnet market cap plus reserve impact alongside the alpha and price estimates; those market-cap figures are derived from the current snapshot and should be treated as estimates.

The pool growth estimator also includes a collapsible compact scenario curve for projected alpha price change versus TAO injected across a fixed 0–2,500 TAO range; when the curve is collapsed, the cards reflow to fill the panel, and when expanded the curve returns beside the stacked result cards on wide screens with a smooth layout transition, hover crosshairs, tooltip readouts, and no point markers on the line for precise inspection.
The scenario chart toggle should render as a mixed-case Show chart / Hide chart pill rather than all caps, so the control reads naturally alongside the rest of the estimator panel.

The pool growth estimator source lives in src/pool-estimator.js and the behavior should stay documented in docs/pool-growth-estimator.md so future edits keep the local constant-product approximation, the implied market-cap output, the fixed-range scenario curve, the hover interaction, and the unavailable-state behavior aligned.
Ctrl/Cmd-clicking a wallet card now opens a wallet transaction modal that combines Taostats extrinsics, transfers, and hotkey stake deltas so the user can inspect the activity behind a wallet change.
The TAO price badge opens a historical TAO/USD modal chart.

Historical metric modals support 24H, 7D, 14D, 30D, and 60D windows, default to 7D on open, include beginner-friendly metric explanations, and can slide by 24 hours with arrow buttons or keyboard shortcuts.

The main dashboard keeps the Latest JSON, History JSON, Recent snapshots, and Latest ingest run inside a collapsible admin panel, and that admin panel is only rendered when TAOSTATS_ADMIN_API_KEY is configured.

When TAOSTATS_ADMIN_API_KEY is configured, the POST admin routes (/api/subnets/:netuid/ingest, /api/subnets/:netuid/backfill, and /api/settings/poll-interval) must receive the matching X-Admin-API-Key header; the browser admin controls already send it.
The admin panel also exposes a browser-triggered backfill form that posts to /api/subnets/:netuid/backfill.
Backfill mode imports subnet history, TAO price history, Tao Flow history, configured wallet balance history, and historical hotkey stake snapshots, then refreshes the live snapshot.
TAO-denominated values can be toggled between TAO and USD using the stored TAO price history.
Missing metric values must remain null in charts and render as gaps, not zeroes.

Current hotkey stake positions are fetched live, and the wallet modal can now show an estimated income-sources section that splits wallet growth into validator/owner buckets for known hotkeys while leaving any untagged remainder in the residual/unclassified bucket.
Subnet stats use a 4-column grid, and collapsible panels use a visible chevron affordance.
